mechanism: Inhibits thioredoxin reductase, increases reactive oxygen species (ROS)
additional_notes: Reported AML cytotoxicity and synergy with other agents in several studies.

mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial translation, impairing mitochondrial function
additional_notes: Reported to selectively impair AML stem/progenitor cells by targeting mitochondrial function.

mechanism: Pleiotropic — reported modulation of Wnt/β-catenin, NF-κB, mTOR pathways
additional_notes: Identified in screens as cytotoxic to several AML lines; multi-target effects reported.

category: Aldehyde dehydrogenase inhibitor / alcohol aversion drug
mechanism: Proposed to induce proteotoxic/oxidative stress and inhibit ALDH (often active with copper)
additional_notes: Often tested with copper to enhance activity; reported AML activity in some studies.

mechanism: Induces endoplasmic reticulum (ER) stress/unfolded protein response (UPR) and apoptosis
additional_notes: Shows anti-leukemia activity in preclinical work.

mechanism: Inhibits the 26S proteasome, leading to accumulation of misfolded proteins and apoptosis
additional_notes: Used clinically in myeloma; evaluated in AML preclinical and some clinical studies, often in combinations.

mechanism: Irreversible inhibitor of Bruton's tyrosine kinase (BTK), blocking BTK signaling
additional_notes: BTK signaling implicated in subsets of AML; preclinical activity reported.

mechanism: Inhibits SRC family kinases, ABL and other kinases affecting signaling pathways
additional_notes: Has activity against some AML signaling pathways and has been tested in AML models.

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1 and JAK2, downregulating JAK/STAT signaling
additional_notes: Of interest in AML cases with JAK/STAT pathway activation and for combination approaches.

mechanism: Inhibits ALK and MET tyrosine kinases (and ROS1), blocking oncogenic signaling
additional_notes: Relevant when targetable rearrangements or dependencies occur in AML.

mechanism: Alters cellular metabolism and AMPK signaling; impacts mitochondrial complex I activity
additional_notes: Reported to sensitize AML cells to other drugs and reduce proliferation.

mechanism: Inhibits HMG-CoA reductase, reducing cholesterol synthesis and affecting prenylation-dependent signaling
additional_notes: Statins have been reported to affect AML cell survival and show synergy with other agents.

mechanism: Inhibits HMG-CoA reductase, impacting cholesterol synthesis and related signaling pathways
additional_notes: Listed alongside atorvastatin as statins of interest for AML; reported synergy in some studies.

mechanism: Reported to inhibit STAT5 signaling and exert anti-leukemic effects in preclinical models
additional_notes: Repurposing candidate identified from preclinical studies.

mechanism: DNA intercalation and modulation of p53 and other signaling pathways
additional_notes: Identified in some AML screens for cytotoxic activity.

mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial translation (and bacterial protein synthesis)
additional_notes: Related mitochondrial-targeting effects reported in AML; used to target leukemic stem cell mitochondrial function.

mechanism: Primary action is serotonin reuptake inhibition; anticancer mechanism in AML screens not fully defined
additional_notes: Reported in some drug-repurposing screens to reduce proliferation in cancer cell lines including hematologic lines.

mechanism: Inhibits Na+/K+ ATPase, altering ion gradients and secondary signaling leading to antiproliferative effects
additional_notes: Cardiac glycosides have shown anti-proliferative effects in some leukemia assays.

mechanism: D2 receptor antagonist; identified in phenotypic screens with cytotoxic activity in AML lines (exact anticancer mechanism unclear)
additional_notes: One of several CNS drugs emerging from phenotypic screens with reported AML line activity.
